notes: distributed: confirmed by real message-sending code, not just dependency presence. src/telegram/send.ts builds TelegramSendOpts/TelegramSendResult and (past the 150-line trim) calls grammy's bot.api.sendMessage/sendPhoto/etc — a durable state change at Telegram. src/line/send.ts:106-153 sendMessageLine() explicitly calls client.replyMessage(...) / client.pushMessage(...) via @line/bot-sdk's messagingApi.MessagingApiClient — included verbatim, not trimmed away. Both are genuine side-effectful remote boundaries per the tag definition (bot messaging). @aws-sdk/client-bedrock (src/agents/bedrock-discovery.ts) is present as a dependency but its usage here is ListFoundationModelsCommand — a READ-ONLY model-catalog discovery call, not itself distributed-tag-qualifying evidence on its own; it's included for completeness (task asked for it as a representative source) but the distributed tag rests on the Telegram/LINE send paths, not on Bedrock. db: confirmed. src/memory/sqlite.ts requires Node's built-in `node:sqlite` module (not an npm sqlite driver) and src/memory/memory-schema.ts runs CREATE TABLE/CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE (fts5) DDL against a `DatabaseSync` handle for meta/files/chunks/embedding-cache/fts tables — this is an owned, embedded, locally-persisted database (moltbot's memory subsystem), not a hosted-API consumer, so it qualifies. The `sqlite-vec` npm dependency (declared in package.json) is a loadable extension for the same embedded database, reinforcing this reading. react: verified absent — `find . -iname '*.tsx' -not -path '*/node_modules/*'` returns 0 hits repo-wide, and tsconfig.json has no `jsx` compiler option set. The `ui/` package (moltbot-control-ui) uses `lit` (Web Components), not React — confirmed via ui/package.json dependencies. test glob: tsconfig.json's exclude list names `src/**/*.test.ts` and `src/**/*.test.tsx` (the .tsx form is dead pattern-matter given 0 tsx files exist) and a real count confirms convention: 885 `*.test.ts` files, 0 `*.spec.ts` files repo-wide — so the test glob is narrowed to `**/*.test.ts` only, dropping the corpus default's `.spec.` and `.tsx` alternatives entirely. REVIEW EDIT: removed react glob key — react not in tags, so no react-class rules install and no react glob belongs in the proposal (convention: default+test always, react iff react tag fires, per-tag keys only when monorepo-scoped).
